name: Phoenix Lights Cover-Up
description: The Phoenix Lights cover-up theory alleges that government agencies deliberately concealed the true nature of a series of aerial phenomena observed over Phoenix, Arizona, and surrounding areas on March 13, 1997. Proponents believe the sightings involved a massive, triangular-shaped unidentified flying object (UFO) of unknown origin, while authorities officially attributed the event to flares dropped during a military training exercise. The alleged cover-up suggests a conspiracy to hide evidence of extraterrestrial visitation or advanced, classified aircraft technology. Evidence cited includes numerous eyewitness accounts describing a large, silent object blocking out stars, inconsistencies in official explanations, and alleged suppression of information. Mainstream perspectives attribute the sightings to misidentification of flares, atmospheric anomalies, or psychological phenomena such as mass hysteria. Critics point to the lack of verifiable physical evidence, conflicting eyewitness testimonies, and the plausibility of the official explanation. The theory persists due to public distrust of government, the enduring fascination with UFOs, and the lack of a universally accepted explanation for the events of that night. The alleged cover-up fuels speculation about secret government projects and the potential for extraterrestrial contact.
subtitle: Hiding the truth about the Phoenix Lights?

claims.claim: The Phoenix Lights were a single, massive UFO.
claims.evidence: Hundreds of eyewitness accounts describe a large, triangular object.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness accounts are subjective and prone to misinterpretation; the 'object' could be a series of separate lights.

claims.claim: The government's flare explanation is a deliberate lie.
claims.evidence: The timing and location of the flares do not match all eyewitness accounts, and some witnesses report a silent, solid object.
claims.criticism: Official investigations confirm the flare deployment; discrepancies in timing and location can be attributed to varying perspectives and recall errors.

claims.claim: The government is concealing evidence of extraterrestrial technology.
claims.evidence: Alleged witness intimidation and suppression of information by government agencies.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence of intimidation; speculation based on anecdotal reports and assumptions about government secrecy.

keyFigures.name: Dr. Lynne Kitei
keyFigures.role: Physician and vocal proponent who documented the events.

keyFigures.name: James McGaha
keyFigures.role: Retired Air Force pilot and astronomer who debunked the UFO claims.

timeline.event: Various explanations and investigations are presented, leading to the development of the cover-up theory.

sources.author: Kitei, Lynne D.
sources.work: The Phoenix Lights: A Skeptic's Discovery That We Are Not Alone
sources.title: Kitei, Lynne D.

sources.author: Davenport, Peter A.
sources.work: National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) Report on the Phoenix Lights
sources.title: Davenport, Peter A.
